title: CLAUDE.md
text: file provides guidance Claude Code (claude.ai/code) working code repository — netaipq sub-package pqverse.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: netaipq networks machine-learning layer pqverse. home feature adds brand-new ML, network, graph, causal-inference, statistical-analysis dependency, keeping ‘MiscMetabar’ lean. also owns genuinely new analysis methods (alpha-diversity estimation, dissimilarity modelling, spatial partitioning, indicator-taxa analysis). Scope guard (ROADMAP.md): ✅ scope: co-occurrence / association networks (cooccur, SpiecEasi, NetCoMi, rMAGMA), bipartite-network viz, DAGs (dagitty), supervised classification (PLS-DA via mixOmics), random-forest / Boruta / SHAP, indicator species & differential abundance (LefSe, ALDEx2 senAnalysis, adiv::dbMANOVAspecies, TITAN2), alpha-diversity estimation (DivNet + breakaway), robust ordination (rpca), dissimilarity & spatial methods (zetadiv, GDM, dbMEM), null-model assembly (NST, iCAMP). ❌ scope: pure ggplot2 helpers (→ ggplotpq), multi-phyloseq comparators (→ comparpq), data-structure utilities (→ tidypq). Dependency rule. netaipq absorbs heavy analysis/ML/network deps. Render resulting plots ggplotpq pure-viz layer already exists; keep analysis wrapper .

title: CLAUDE.md
code: # Run code with loaded package Rscript -e "devtools::load_all(); code"  # Run all tests Rscript -e "devtools::test()"  # Run tests for files starting with {name} Rscript -e "devtools::test(filter = '^{name}')"  # Generate documentation Rscript -e "devtools::document()"  # Full package check Rscript -e "devtools::check()"

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Use base pipe (|>) magrittr (%>%) Use function() {} anonymous functions (\() multi-statement) Tests R/{name}.R go tests/testthat/test_{name}.R (underscore) Every user-facing function must exported full roxygen2 documentation (@param, @return, @export, @examples, @author) Wrap roxygen comments 80 characters CRAN example constraints: primary example \donttest{}, variants \dontrun{}; cap per-sample work 5 samples via prune_samples(sample_names(data_fungi_mini)[1:5], data_fungi_mini) filter abundant taxa network/ordination examples Guard every Suggests-package call requireNamespace() + cli::cli_abort() Air format package: air format . (scope diff — revert incidental reformats unrelated files)

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Workspace CLAUDE.md: pqverse/CLAUDE.md (overall context) ROADMAP section: https://github.com/adrientaudiere/pqverse/ROADMAP.md#netaipq Sister packages: pqverse_pkg/MiscMetabar/, pqverse_pkg/ggplotpq/, pqverse_pkg/comparpq/, pqverse_pkg/tidypq/

title: Get started with netaipq
text: netaipq pqverse home networks machine-learning analyses phyloseq objects: co-occurrence association networks, bipartite-network visualisation, supervised classification, indicator-species differential-abundance methods, genuinely new analysis methods go beyond simple ggplot2 wrapper. absorbing feature add brand-new machine-learning, network, graph, statistical-analysis dependency, netaipq keeps MiscMetabar lean. Pure ggplot2 visualisation belongs ggplotpq; cross-study comparators belong comparpq.

title: Get started with netaipq
Get started with netaipq
text: bipartite network links two kinds nodes — samples taxa — edge whenever taxon occurs sample. bipartite_network_pq() builds draws network phyloseq object. readable figure take samples commonest taxa data_fungi_mini (shipped MiscMetabar).  Taxa nodes coloured Phylum; edges connect taxon samples observed . function returns ggplot object, can post-process ggplot2 layer theme — example provided ggplotpq.
code: library(netaipq) data(data_fungi_mini)  ps <- prune_samples(sample_names(data_fungi_mini)[1:5], data_fungi_mini) ps <- clean_pq(ps, silent = TRUE) top_taxa <- names(sort(taxa_sums(ps), decreasing = TRUE))[1:20] ps <- prune_taxa(top_taxa, ps) ps #> phyloseq-class experiment-level object #> otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 12 taxa and 5 samples ] #> sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 5 samples by 7 sample variables ] #> tax_table()   Taxonomy Table:    [ 12 taxa by 12 taxonomic ranks ] #> refseq()      DNAStringSet:      [ 12 reference sequences ] bipartite_network_pq(ps, taxa_color = "Phylum", seed = 1)

title: Get started with netaipq
text: function reference lists every argument bipartite_network_pq(), including node sizing, layout, label control. netaipq active development: network, machine-learning, causal-inference methods added. See ROADMAP coming next.

what: Networks and Machine Learning for phyloseq Objects
title: Networks and Machine Learning for phyloseq Objects
text: netaipq pqverse home networks machine-learning analyses phyloseq objects: co-occurrence association networks, bipartite-network visualisation, supervised classification, indicator-species differential-abundance methods, genuinely new analysis methods go beyond simple ggplot2 wrapper. absorbing feature add brand-new machine-learning, network, graph, statistical-analysis dependency, netaipq keeps MiscMetabar lean. Pure ggplot2 visualisation belongs ggplotpq; cross-study comparators belong comparpq.

title: Networks and Machine Learning for phyloseq Objects
text: can install development version netaipq GitHub :

title: Networks and Machine Learning for phyloseq Objects
code: library(netaipq) data(data_fungi_mini)  # A readable subset: a few samples and the commonest taxa ps <- prune_samples(sample_names(data_fungi_mini)[1:5], data_fungi_mini) ps <- clean_pq(ps, silent = TRUE) top_taxa <- names(sort(taxa_sums(ps), decreasing = TRUE))[1:20] ps <- prune_taxa(top_taxa, ps)  bipartite_network_pq(ps, taxa_color = "Phylum", seed = 1)

what: Bipartite network of samples and taxa from a phyloseq object — bipartite_network_pq
title: Bipartite network of samples and taxa from a phyloseq object — bipartite_network_pq
text: Draw bipartite network samples taxa nodes edges link sample taxon whenever taxon occurs sample (.e. corresponding cell OTU table greater min_abundance). Node positions computed igraph result rendered static ggplot2 object (htmlwidgets), can customised usual + syntax. Inspired Figure 2 Taudière et al. (2018) vda-lab/snowflake project. Sample-based taxa-based metrics can mapped node colour node size aesthetics.

title: Bipartite network of samples and taxa from a phyloseq object — bipartite_network_pq
code: bipartite_network_pq(   physeq,   taxa_color = NULL,   sample_color = "grey20",   taxa_size = "abundance",   sample_size = "abundance",   min_abundance = 0,   layout = "fr",   projection = NULL,   ordination = "nmds",   edge_color = "grey70",   edge_alpha = 0.3,   edge_weight = "none",   edge_trans = "log10",   point_size = 4,   sample_label = FALSE,   taxa_label = FALSE,   label_size_sample = 3,   label_size_taxa = 3,   na_remove = FALSE,   seed = NULL )

title: Bipartite network of samples and taxa from a phyloseq object — bipartite_network_pq
text: physeq (required) phyloseq-class object obtained using phyloseq package. taxa_color (default NULL) Name tax_table column used colour taxa nodes. NULL, taxa nodes share single colour. sample_color (default "grey20") Controls colour sample nodes. Either name sam_data column colour samples — numeric column gives continuous viridis gradient, factor character column discrete viridis palette, drawn scale stays independent taxa_color — single colour (name hex) applied every sample node. value matching sam_data column name treated column, otherwise colour. taxa_size (default "abundance") Metric mapped size taxa nodes. One "abundance" (total number sequences), "prevalence" (number samples taxon occurs) "none" (constant size). sample_size (default "abundance") Metric mapped size sample nodes. One "abundance" (sequencing depth), "richness" (number taxa observed) "none" (constant size). min_abundance (int, default 0) edge drawn sample taxon corresponding OTU-table value strictly greater threshold. layout (default "fr") igraph layout algorithm used position nodes projection NULL. One "fr" (Fruchterman-Reingold), "kk" (Kamada-Kawai), "bipartite" (two parallel rows nodes) "nicely" (igraph's automatic choice). Ignored projection set. projection (default NULL) Position nodes distances among sample nodes reflect community dissimilarity, instead abstract igraph layout. Either vegan::vegdist() dissimilarity method name (e.g. "bray", "jaccard"; samples ordinated two dimensions, see ordination) precomputed sample projection. latter may numeric matrix sample row names, data frame / tibble sample names row names one column, coordinates axis-name pair (x_umap/y_umap — MiscMetabar::umap_pq() output works directly —, NMDS1/NMDS2, PC1/PC2, Axis.1/Axis.2, ...) first two numeric columns. every case taxon node placed abundance-weighted average samples occurs (vegan::wascores()). Requires vegan package. ordination (default "nmds") Ordination used project dissimilarity matrix two dimensions projection distance method name. One "nmds" (non-metric multidimensional scaling, via vegan::metaMDS()) "pcoa" (principal coordinates analysis, via stats::cmdscale()). Ignored projection coordinate matrix. edge_color (default "grey70") Colour edges. edge_weight encodes weight colour ("color" ""), edge_color defines gradient: single colour gives gradient "grey70" (fewest sequences) colour (sequences), length-2 vector gives gradient two colours (low high). edges colour-weighted, last value used uniform edge colour. edge_alpha (float, [0:1], default 0.3) Opacity edges. edge_weight (default "none") Weight edges number sequences (OTU-table value sample-taxon pair). One "none" (uniform edges), "linewidth" (thicker edges sequences, legend), "color" (darker-grey edges sequences) "". edge_trans (default "log10") Transformation applied per-edge sequence count mapped edge aesthetic(s). One "log10" "identity". point_size (default 4) Base point size, used directly corresponding *_size argument "none" upper bound size scale otherwise. sample_label (logical, default FALSE) Print sample names next sample nodes. Either single TRUE/FALSE (label none) logical vector length number samples physeq (phyloseq::sample_names() order) label subset. taxa_label (logical, default FALSE) Print taxa names next taxa nodes. Either single TRUE/FALSE (label none) logical vector length number taxa physeq (phyloseq::taxa_names() order) label subset. label_size_sample (default 3) Text size sample-node labels. label_size_taxa (default 3) Text size taxa-node labels. na_remove (logical, default FALSE) TRUE, samples NA sample_color removed plotting. seed (int, default NULL) Optional seed passed set.seed() make (stochastic) layout reproducible.

title: Bipartite network of samples and taxa from a phyloseq object — bipartite_network_pq
text: ggplot object.

title: Bipartite network of samples and taxa from a phyloseq object — bipartite_network_pq
text: Adrien Taudière

title: Bipartite network of samples and taxa from a phyloseq object — bipartite_network_pq
code: if (requireNamespace("igraph")) {   # Keep the plot readable: a handful of samples and the commonest taxa   ps <- prune_samples(sample_names(data_fungi_mini)[1:20], data_fungi_mini)   ps <- clean_pq(ps, silent = TRUE)   bipartite_network_pq(ps, taxa_color = "Class", seed = 1) }  # \donttest{   ps <- prune_samples(sample_names(data_fungi_mini)[1:20], data_fungi_mini)   ps <- clean_pq(ps, silent = TRUE)   # Sample nodes positioned by Bray-Curtis dissimilarity; each taxon sits at   # the weighted average of the samples it occurs in.   bipartite_network_pq(ps, projection = "bray", seed = 1)   # Position the samples from a precomputed UMAP (needs the `umap` package).   ps <- prune_samples(sample_names(data_fungi_mini)[1:20], data_fungi_mini)   ps <- clean_pq(ps, silent = TRUE)   df_umap <- MiscMetabar::umap_pq(ps, seed = 1) #> Taxa are now in columns. #> Taxa are now in rows. #> ! what: Probabilistic species co-occurrence from a phyloseq object — cooccurrence_network_pq
title: Probabilistic species co-occurrence from a phyloseq object — cooccurrence_network_pq
text: Compute probabilistic species co-occurrence model Veech (2013) taxa phyloseq object using cooccur::cooccur(). pair taxa classified positively associated, negatively associated randomly associated given observed co-occurrence across samples. default OTU table converted presence/absence MiscMetabar::as_binary_otu_table() analysis: cooccur binary (incidence) model gives spurious results raw abundance data.

title: Probabilistic species co-occurrence from a phyloseq object — cooccurrence_network_pq
code: cooccurrence_network_pq(   physeq,   binary = TRUE,   n_taxa = NULL,   spp_names = TRUE,   prob = "comb",   thresh = TRUE,   ... )

title: Probabilistic species co-occurrence from a phyloseq object — cooccurrence_network_pq
text: physeq (required) phyloseq-class object obtained using phyloseq package. binary (logical, default TRUE) Convert OTU table presence/absence analysis. Keep TRUE unless know data suit (rarely appropriate) abundance behaviour cooccur. n_taxa (int, default NULL) set, keep n_taxa prevalent taxa analysis. cooccur scales quadratically number taxa, capping recommended rich datasets. spp_names (logical, default TRUE) Passed cooccur::cooccur(): keep taxa names output. prob (default "comb") probability method passed cooccur::cooccur(), either "comb" (combinatorics) "hyper" (hypergeometric). thresh (logical, default TRUE) Passed cooccur::cooccur(): remove taxa pairs expected co-occur less one time. ... parameters passed cooccur::cooccur().

title: Probabilistic species co-occurrence from a phyloseq object — cooccurrence_network_pq
text: object class cooccur (see cooccur::cooccur()). Use cooccur::effect.sizes(), cooccur::prob.table() summary() explore , plot() visualise association matrix.

title: Probabilistic species co-occurrence from a phyloseq object — cooccurrence_network_pq
text: Veech, J. . (2013) probabilistic model analysing species co-occurrence. doi:10.1111/j.1466-8238.2012.00789.x .

title: Probabilistic species co-occurrence from a phyloseq object — cooccurrence_network_pq
text: Adrien Taudière

title: Probabilistic species co-occurrence from a phyloseq object — cooccurrence_network_pq

what: Test a causal DAG against phyloseq sample data — dag_test_pq
title: Test a causal DAG against phyloseq sample data — dag_test_pq
text: Confront hypothesised causal directed acyclic graph (DAG) sample data phyloseq object. implied conditional independencies DAG tested data dagitty::localTests(): independence DAG claims checked, large estimate signals DAG inconsistent data. Diversity descriptors samples (nb_seq, sequencing depth, nb_otu, observed richness) added sample data MiscMetabar::add_info_to_sam_data() can used DAG nodes alongside columns sam_data.

title: Test a causal DAG against phyloseq sample data — dag_test_pq
code: dag_test_pq(   physeq,   dag,   as_factor = NULL,   type = "cis.chisq",   na_remove = TRUE,   ... )

title: Test a causal DAG against phyloseq sample data — dag_test_pq
text: physeq (required) phyloseq-class object obtained using phyloseq package. dag (required) causal DAG, either dagitty object dagitty model string (e.g. "dag { Time -> nb_otu nb_seq -> nb_otu }"). Every node DAG must column (enriched) sample data. as_factor (character vector, default NULL) Names DAG variables coerce factor test (useful categorical metadata stored numbers characters). type (default "cis.chisq") independence-test type passed dagitty::localTests(). See help available options. na_remove (logical, default TRUE) Remove samples missing value DAG variable test. ... parameters passed dagitty::localTests().

title: Test a causal DAG against phyloseq sample data — dag_test_pq
text: data frame returned dagitty::localTests(), one row per implied conditional independence, estimate confidence interval. Pass dagitty::plotLocalTestResults() visualise .

title: Test a causal DAG against phyloseq sample data — dag_test_pq
text: Ankan, ., Wortel, . M. N. & Textor, J. (2021) Testing graphical causal models using R package "dagitty". doi:10.1002/cpz1.45 .

title: Test a causal DAG against phyloseq sample data — dag_test_pq
text: Adrien Taudière

title: Test a causal DAG against phyloseq sample data — dag_test_pq
code: # \donttest{ if (requireNamespace("dagitty", quietly = TRUE)) {   dag <- "dag {     Time -> nb_otu     nb_seq -> nb_otu     Time -> Height     Height -> nb_otu   }"   dag_test_pq(data_fungi_mini, dag, as_factor = c("Time", "Height")) } #>                     rmsea       x2  df   p.value rmsea 2.5% rmsea 97.5% #> Hght _||_ nb_s 0.00000000 123.3229 124 0.5002821 0.08891009   0.1397312 #> Time _||_ nb_s 0.02776546 196.6110 186 0.2828356 0.09810813   0.1389691 # } if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ if (requireNamespace("dagitty", quietly = TRUE)) {   # Add Hill number q = 2 (inverse Simpson) to sample data   hill2 <- phyloseq::estimate_richness(data_fungi_mini, measures = "InvSimpson")   phyloseq::sample_data(data_fungi_mini)$hill_2 <- hill2$InvSimpson   dag2 <- "dag {     Time -> hill_2     nb_seq -> hill_2     Diameter -> hill_2     Time -> Diameter   }"   dag_test_pq(data_fungi_mini, dag2, as_factor = "Time") } } # }

what: Microbial correlation network of a phyloseq object using ggClusterNet — ggclusternet_pq
title: Microbial correlation network of a phyloseq object using ggClusterNet — ggclusternet_pq
text: Build per-group microbial correlation network phyloseq object ggClusterNet::network.2() (Wen et al. 2022). level group, taxa correlated, thresholded correlation strength significance, laid one ggClusterNet network layouts returned together ggplot2 rendering.

title: Microbial correlation network of a phyloseq object using ggClusterNet — ggclusternet_pq
code: ggclusternet_pq(   physeq,   group,   n = 500,   r_threshold = 0.6,   p_threshold = 0.05,   maxnode = 5,   layout_net = "model_maptree2",   big = TRUE,   select_layout = TRUE,   label = FALSE,   zipi = FALSE,   ... )

title: Microbial correlation network of a phyloseq object using ggClusterNet — ggclusternet_pq
text: physeq (required) phyloseq-class object obtained using phyloseq package. group (required) Name sam_data column defining groups separate network built. n (int, default 500) Number -abundant taxa kept analysis (N argument ggClusterNet::network.2()). r_threshold (float, default 0.6) Minimum absolute correlation edge kept. p_threshold (float, default 0.05) Maximum p-value edge kept. maxnode (int, default 5) Maximum node size layout. layout_net (default "model_maptree2") ggClusterNet layout algorithm. big (logical, default TRUE) Use large-network code path ggClusterNet::network.2(). select_layout (logical, default TRUE) Let ggClusterNet adjust layout. label (logical, default FALSE) Draw taxa labels network. zipi (logical, default FALSE) Compute within/among-module connectivity (Zi-Pi) classification. ... parameters passed ggClusterNet::network.2().

title: Microbial correlation network of a phyloseq object using ggClusterNet — ggclusternet_pq
text: list returned ggClusterNet::network.2(), whose first element ggplot2 network plot whose remaining elements hold network correlation data.

title: Microbial correlation network of a phyloseq object using ggClusterNet — ggclusternet_pq
text: Wen, T. et al. (2022) ggClusterNet: R package microbiome network analysis modularity-based multiple network layouts. doi:10.1002/imt2.32 .

title: Microbial correlation network of a phyloseq object using ggClusterNet — ggclusternet_pq
text: Adrien Taudière

title: Microbial correlation network of a phyloseq object using ggClusterNet — ggclusternet_pq
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # `ggClusterNet` is a GitHub package and needs WGCNA (Bioconductor): # remotes::install_github("taowenmicro/ggClusterNet") # pak::pkg_install("WGCNA") res <- ggclusternet_pq(data_fungi_mini, group = "Height", n = 200) res[[1]] } # }

what: netaipq: Networks and Machine Learning for 'phyloseq' Objects — netaipq-package
title: netaipq: Networks and Machine Learning for 'phyloseq' Objects — netaipq-package
text: Network, graph, causal-inference, machine-learning tools 'phyloseq' objects within 'pqverse' ecosystem. Provides genuinely new analysis methods (co-occurrence association networks, bipartite-network visualisation, supervised classification, indicator-species differential-abundance analyses) go beyond simple 'ggplot2' wrappers. 'netaipq' home item adding brand-new machine-learning, network, graph, statistical-analysis dependency, keeping 'MiscMetabar' lean. Built top 'MiscMetabar'.

title: netaipq: Networks and Machine Learning for 'phyloseq' Objects — netaipq-package
text: Maintainer: Adrien Taudière adrien.taudiere@zaclys.net (ORCID) [copyright holder] Authors: Adrien Taudière adrien.taudiere@zaclys.net (ORCID) [copyright holder]

what: Species-association network of a phyloseq object using netassoc — netassoc_network_pq
title: Species-association network of a phyloseq object using netassoc — netassoc_network_pq
text: Infer species-association network phyloseq object netassoc::make_netassoc_network(). method (Morueta-Holme et al. 2016) compares observed co-occurrence structure null model random assembly keeps taxa pairs whose association departs significantly expectation, returning partial-correlation network taxa.

title: Species-association network of a phyloseq object using netassoc — netassoc_network_pq
code: netassoc_network_pq(   physeq,   numnulls = 100,   method = "partial_correlation",   plot = FALSE,   verbose = FALSE,   ... )

title: Species-association network of a phyloseq object using netassoc — netassoc_network_pq
text: physeq (required) phyloseq-class object obtained using phyloseq package. numnulls (int, default 100) Number null-model replicates used build expectation. Increase real analyses; default keeps examples tests fast. method (default "partial_correlation") Association measure passed netassoc::make_netassoc_network(). plot (logical, default FALSE) Draw heatmaps produced netassoc::make_netassoc_network(). FALSE default avoid plotting side effect; returned object still contains networks. verbose (logical, default FALSE) Passed netassoc::make_netassoc_network(). ... parameters passed netassoc::make_netassoc_network().

title: Species-association network of a phyloseq object using netassoc — netassoc_network_pq
text: list returned netassoc::make_netassoc_network(). network_all element igraph graph significant associations.

title: Species-association network of a phyloseq object using netassoc — netassoc_network_pq
text: Morueta-Holme, N. et al. (2016) network approach inferring species associations co-occurrence data. doi:10.1111/ecog.01892 .

title: Species-association network of a phyloseq object using netassoc — netassoc_network_pq
text: Adrien Taudière

title: Species-association network of a phyloseq object using netassoc — netassoc_network_pq
code: if (requireNamespace("netassoc", quietly = TRUE)) {   ps <- prune_samples(sample_names(data_fungi_mini)[1:5], data_fungi_mini)   ps <- clean_pq(ps, silent = TRUE)   top_taxa <- names(sort(taxa_sums(ps), decreasing = TRUE))[1:20]   ps <- prune_taxa(top_taxa, ps)   res <- netassoc_network_pq(ps, numnulls = 20)    netassoc::plot_netassoc_network(res$network_all) } #> . #> Warning: 1 instances of variables with zero scale detected! #> Warning: 1 instances of variables with zero scale detected! #> .. #> Warning: 1 instances of variables with zero scale detected! #> Warning: 1 instances of variables with zero scale detected! #> .... #> Warning: 1 instances of variables with zero scale detected! #> Warning: 1 instances of variables with zero scale detected! #> .. #> Warning: 1 instances of variables with zero scale detected! #> Warning: 1 instances of variables with zero scale detected! #> .... #> Warning: 1 instances of variables with zero scale detected! #> Warning: 1 instances of variables with zero scale detected! #> ...... #> Warning: 1 instances of variables with zero scale detected! #> Warning: 1 instances of variables with zero scale detected! #> . #> Warning: Non-positive edge weight found, ignoring all weights during graph layout.

what: Compositional taxa-environment correlation with SparCEV — sparcev_pq
title: Compositional taxa-environment correlation with SparCEV — sparcev_pq
text: Compute sparse correlation taxon continuous environmental (sample) variable using CompoCor::SparCEV() (Sparse Correlation Environmental Variable). SparCEV accounts compositional nature metabarcoding count data flags taxa whose abundance significantly correlated variable.

title: Compositional taxa-environment correlation with SparCEV — sparcev_pq
Compositional taxa-environment correlation with SparCEV — sparcev_pq
text: physeq (required) phyloseq-class object obtained using phyloseq package. variable (required) Name continuous variable sam_data slot correlate taxa . add_tax_table (logical, default TRUE) Join tax_table physeq result taxon carries taxonomy. ... parameters passed CompoCor::SparCEV().

title: Compositional taxa-environment correlation with SparCEV — sparcev_pq
text: data frame one row per taxon: taxa (taxon name), cor (SparCEV correlation variable) correlated (logical, TRUE correlation deemed significant). add_tax_table = TRUE columns tax_table appended. rendering result (e.g. labelled scatter plot) belongs ggplotpq.

title: Compositional taxa-environment correlation with SparCEV — sparcev_pq
text: Adrien Taudière

title: Compositional taxa-environment correlation with SparCEV — sparcev_pq
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # `CompoCor` is a GitHub package: # remotes::install_github("IbTJensen/CompoCor") ps <- subset_samples(data_fungi_mini, !is.na(Time)) res <- sparcev_pq(ps, variable = "Time")  res |>    filter(correlated) |>    ggplot(aes(x = cor, y = Family, shape = Guild, color = Order)) +    geom_point() +    geom_text(aes(label = taxa), nudge_y = 0.3, size = 2.5) } # }

title: netaipq 0.0.0.9000
text: Initial development version package, providing networks machine-learning tools ‘phyloseq’ objects within ‘pqverse’ ecosystem. Add “Get started netaipq” vignette pkgdown website skeleton. bipartite_network_pq() draws static ‘ggplot2’ bipartite network samples taxa nodes edges come OTU table, sample- taxa-based metrics mappable node colour size, optional sample taxa name labels via sample_label taxa_label (single TRUE/FALSE per-node logical vector). sample_color accepts either sam_data column colour samples (numeric gives continuous gradient, factor character discrete palette, scale independent taxa_color) single fixed colour. Node positions can reflect community dissimilarity projection (vegan::vegdist() method ordinated ordination, precomputed 2D sample projection output NMDS MiscMetabar::umap_pq()), taxon placed abundance-weighted average samples occurs . Edges can weighted number sequences (log10-transformed default) encoded line width, grey colour gradient, via edge_weight edge_trans. cooccurrence_network_pq() computes probabilistic species co-occurrence model Veech (2013) taxa ‘phyloseq’ object via cooccur::cooccur(), converting OTU table presence/absence default. dag_test_pq() confronts hypothesised causal DAG (diversity-enriched) sample data ‘phyloseq’ object using dagitty::localTests(). ggclusternet_pq() builds per-group microbial correlation network ggClusterNet::network.2(). netassoc_network_pq() infers species-association network ‘phyloseq’ object null model random assembly via netassoc::make_netassoc_network(). sparcev_pq() correlates taxon continuous environmental variable accounting compositionality via CompoCor::SparCEV().
